hypothesis: Incorporating episodic memory of past simulation failures and parameter extraction errors into the multi-agent framework—specifically by maintaining a structured, queryable log of which force field table layouts caused misassignment errors and which simulation configurations produced incorrect outputs—will enable agents to proactively apply corrective heuristics on future tasks involving structurally similar papers or simulation types, measurably reducing error rates (e.g., improving IoU scores for unconventionally formatted force fields like EPM2 from 0.67 toward ≥0.95) compared to the memoryless baseline system evaluated in this paper.
